title: Wind Farms: A Heartbreaking Change for Some
summary: In Wales, people are talking about building big wind turbines to make clean energy. Grace lives near Abercarn and loves the open land with birds and animals. She already sees eight wind turbines from her window and supports renewable energy. But new plans might add up to 20 more turbines, some very tall, which could change the landscape a lot. Grace is worried this will hurt the natural beauty she enjoys. Many people in Wales have shared their thoughts with the BBC, with some supporting wind farms for clean energy and others, like Grace, concerned about the impact on their communities. The Welsh government wants to use only renewable energy by 2035, so this debate is important for the future.

background_read: Wind farms are groups of tall turbines that catch wind to make electricity without pollution. In Wales, the government wants to use only clean energy like this by 2035 to help the environment. Some people like Grace worry that building more turbines could harm the natural beauty and wildlife where they live. This article shows how communities are divided between supporting clean energy and protecting their local landscapes.
Article_Structure: WHO: Grace, a retired geologist living in Abercarn, Wales, and other people in Wales sharing their views with the BBC.
WHAT: Plans to build new wind turbines in Wales, which could change the landscape and cause debate between supporting renewable energy and protecting communities.
WHERE: Abercarn in Caerphilly county, Wales, and other areas across Wales.
WHY: To discuss the conflict between achieving renewable energy goals and addressing concerns about environmental and community impacts from wind farm development.

perspectives.description: Some people in Wales support wind farms because they provide clean energy and help fight climate change.
Others, like Grace, worry that large wind turbines could damage the natural beauty and wildlife in their areas, affecting their way of life.
